title: Interior Project Management Software | SiteFlow
metaDescription: Try SiteFlow, #1 Interior Software. Manage multiple interior projects easily with SiteFlow interior project ERP solution
author: SiteFlow Operations Team

Why SiteFlow
How SiteFlow Helps Interior Companies Run Tighter Projects
Interior work is fast, messy, and client-facing. The margin for error is thin. Here is what changes when you move from spreadsheets and WhatsApp to SiteFlow.
LEFT: Problem → Solution rows
Before Vendor bills arrive and get paid. Nobody checks whether the work was actually done or matches the purchase order. Disputes happen later when it’s too late to recover the money.
With SiteFlow Every vendor bill is matched to a purchase order and delivery record before it can be approved. Multi-level sign-offs mean nothing gets paid without the right person seeing it first.
Before Scope grows through WhatsApp messages and verbal agreements. By the time you calculate the actual project cost, you’re already over budget with no documentation to back a change order.
With SiteFlow Quotations, BOQs, and scope items are tracked digitally from day one. Every change is logged with a timestamp. Client approval records stay in the system, not in a chat thread.
Before Labour attendance is marked manually by a supervisor. Payroll is calculated at month end from a register that may or may not reflect what actually happened on site.
With SiteFlow GPS-tagged attendance and face recognition replace the register entirely. Salary calculations run from verified attendance data — no manual reconciliation, no disputes.
Before You find out the project made a loss after handover, when all the bills are in. There is no way to see whether you are heading for trouble mid-project while there is still time to act.
With SiteFlow Live project P&L updates as bills come in and work progresses. You see margin erosion the week it happens — not the month after the project closes.
RIGHT: Proof card

Manual vs Digital
WhatsApp and Excel Cannot Run an Interior Project
They work fine for one project. The moment you have three running, six vendors to pay, and a client asking for an update, they stop working.
BAD PANEL
📱 How it looks right now
Flooring bill ₹2.1L — please approve
ok paid
Marble change — doing Italian instead
sure manage it
⚠ Month-end: project ₹3.2L over budget
✕
WhatsApp + Excel + Registers
How most interior firms run today
✕
Vendor Bills
Bills approved on WhatsApp. Nobody checks if the work was done or matches the PO. The loss shows up at the end.
✕
Project Cost
Total cost is visible only after handover when every bill is finally in. Overruns have already happened.
✕
Scope Changes
Client asks for changes verbally. Nothing is written. At billing time, they dispute the extra amount because there is no record.
✕
Labour Attendance
Supervisor marks a register. Payroll calculated manually. Workers who were absent still get counted — disputes every month.
✕
Material Tracking
Materials ordered on call, tracked loosely. Leftover stock discovered after handover — the over-ordering loss is already done.
✕
Client Updates
WhatsApp photos with no standard format. Different clients get different information. Confidence drops fast.
GOOD PANEL

Platform Features
Everything Interior Firms Need. One Platform.
The three modules below cover where interior projects most commonly lose money: progress, materials, and unverified labour costs.
ROW 1: Progress Tracking
📋 Progress Tracking
Take Control of your Project
SiteFlow offers real-time updates on task progress, milestones, and activities, helping projects stay on schedule and within budget. Visual documentation gives everyone the same picture of what is happening on site.
✓
Instant updates from site without waiting for calls or messages
✓
Task and progress tracking tied to actual milestone completion
✓
Visual documentation with geo-tagged photos and timestamps
✓
Automated consolidated reports for clients and management
See this in a demo →

FAQ
FAQ
Questions about SiteFlow for Interior Firms
If you do not see your question here, book a demo and our team will walk you through it live.
Book Free Demo →
Yes. SiteFlow works for any project type. Residential firms use it for scope tracking, client approvals, and vendor billing. Commercial fit-out companies use it for multi-floor progress tracking, larger procurement cycles, and subcontractor management. There is no minimum project size to get started.
Scope items and change requests are logged in the system with timestamps. When a client requests a change, it is recorded with the date and the agreed scope. This creates a clear paper trail that both parties can refer to at billing time. Most interior billing disputes happen because there is no written record of what was agreed. SiteFlow removes that problem.
Yes. You get a single dashboard showing all active projects with their progress, costs, pending bills, and attendance. Material libraries and vendor lists are shared across projects so you do not set them up from scratch each time. Our interior clients typically run between 2 and 15 projects simultaneously.
Most interior companies go live within 1 to 3 days. Your dedicated onboarding manager loads your material library, rate library, vendor master, and active project details before your team logs in. No IT team needed. Training runs in short module-wise sessions in Hindi or English, and your manager stays reachable for the first 30 days.
Yes. Interior projects typically have carpenters, painters, electricians, and plumbers from different agencies on the same site. SiteFlow tracks attendance separately for each worker regardless of who they work for. GPS location and face recognition apply to all of them. Payroll calculations run per worker from verified attendance data, not supervisor estimates.
Yes. SiteFlow has a two-way integration with Tally ERP and Zoho Books. Purchase orders, vendor invoices, and client receipts sync automatically. Your accounts team stays on Tally while your site team works on SiteFlow. No double entry, no manual exports.
Interior project management software is a platform that helps interior design firms manage their projects from quotation to client handover in one place. It covers scope tracking, vendor billing, material management, labour attendance, and project profit visibility. Interior designers need it because managing three or more projects simultaneously using WhatsApp, Excel, and phone calls leads to billing disputes, cost overruns, and missed deadlines that are only discovered after the project ends.
SiteFlow is purpose-built for construction and interior companies in India and the Middle East. Unlike generic project management tools such as Asana, Monday, or Notion, SiteFlow includes construction-specific modules: GPS attendance with face recognition, material tracking with PO matching, vendor billing approval workflows, project P&L linked to daily site activity, and Tally integration.
Generic tools are built for software teams, marketing agencies, and office-based workflows. They do not handle purchase orders, vendor bills, GPS attendance, material stock levels, or construction-specific billing like retention and RA bills. SiteFlow is built specifically for site-based businesses where the work happens on location, not in a browser.
Accurate cost tracking for interior projects requires three things working together: every vendor bill matched to a purchase order before payment, material usage logged against a project budget rather than a shared sheet, and labour costs calculated from verified attendance rather than supervisor estimates. SiteFlow connects all three into a single system so the project P&L updates in real time.
Yes, and this is one of the most common reasons interior firms switch to SiteFlow. Most billing disputes happen because scope changes were agreed verbally and not documented. When a client denies approving a change that added cost, there is no record to refer to. SiteFlow logs every scope item and change order with a timestamp and links it to the final invoice.
Yes. The SiteFlow mobile app is built for Android phones and designed for field use. Site engineers submit daily progress reports, mark attendance, upload site photos, and raise material requests from the app. It works on low-end Android devices and has offline capability for sites with poor connectivity.
The minimum a capable interior project management platform needs: scope and BOQ management tied to client quotations, vendor bill approval with PO matching, material tracking per project, GPS or biometric attendance for labour, client invoicing linked to milestone completion, and a live project P&L that updates as bills and payments come in.
SiteFlow is priced as an annual subscription with no setup fees, no per-module charges, and no implementation costs. Onboarding, training, and WhatsApp support are included in the subscription. See the SiteFlow pricing page for current plans, or book a demo to get a quote for your specific setup.
